title: ancylobothrys petersiana
intro: Ancylobothrys petersiana, also known as the yellow milkwood or yellow milk-bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ancylobothrys petersiana include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a remedy for snake bites and other poisonous bites. Herbal preparations of Ancylobothrys petersiana can be made by infusing the leaves in water or making a decoction, and it is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
